info.name: CodeKnow
info.description: CodeKnow codebase intelligence API — local dev collection. Base URL points to the FastAPI backend on port 8080. All endpoints live under the /codeknow prefix.

variable.description: JWT from the GitHub login flow. Set automatically by the login request's test script, or paste manually.

request.description:
This endpoint returns a 307 redirect to GitHub's OAuth consent screen. It is meant to run in a browser, not respond with JSON — so open this URL directly in your browser:

http://localhost:8080/codeknow/auth/github/login

After authorizing, GitHub redirects back to /codeknow/auth/github/callback, which mints a JWT. Make sure the callback URL is registered in your GitHub OAuth App settings:

http://localhost:8080/codeknow/auth/github/callback

query.description: Temporary code from GitHub after user authorizes.
Signed state returned alongside the code (CSRF protection).

request.description:
Exchange an OAuth code for a CodeKnow JWT.

How to get code + state for local testing:
1. Open {{baseUrl}}{{prefix}}/auth/github/login in your browser.
2. Authorize the app.
3. After redirect, your browser lands on the callback URL with ?code=...&state=... in the address bar — copy both values into this request.

If FRONTEND_REDIRECT is set, the callback returns a 307 redirect instead of JSON (see backend .env.example). For API testing, leave FRONTEND_REDIRECT blank so this returns JSON.

On success, the response body contains access_token, token_type, and user. The test script below auto-saves the token into the {{token}} collection variable.

exec: pm.test('JWT saved to collection variable', function () {
console.log('No access_token in response. Body:', JSON.stringify(json));
console.log('Could not parse response as JSON:', e.message);

header.description: CodeKnow JWT from the GitHub login flow. Uses the {{token}} collection variable.

request.description:
Analyze a GitHub repository's contributor map and bus factor. Requires a valid Bearer JWT.

Accepts repo_url in either form:
- https://github.com/owner/repo
- owner/repo

Note: fetches up to 500 recent commits by default and concurrently pulls each commit's detail — large repos may take a few seconds. Only github.com is supported today.

response.name: Bad URL (400)
